skill.name: content-research-writer
skill.description: Guides writers through research-backed outlining, drafting, citation management, hook improvement, and section-by-section revision.
skill.summary: Helps create researched articles, newsletters, tutorials, and thought leadership drafts with citations and editorial feedback.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby or shell backtick patterns, but review found markdown examples and benign local setup commands, not executable skill code. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by ordinary prose at SKILL.md lines 3, 84, and 362. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network code, or hidden execution logic was found.

low_findings.title: Benign Local Shell Setup Example
low_findings.description: The only real shell commands are user-facing setup examples that create and enter a writing folder and create an empty draft file. They are not hidden execution logic, do not include user-controlled interpolation, and do not contact external systems.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible markdown examples for mkdir, cd, and touch. They perform local file setup only and have no command injection or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Backtick Execution Alerts
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick alerts mostly point to fenced markdown examples, prompt examples, and formatted output templates. No Ruby source, dynamic command execution, or code path executing those markdown blocks was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are documentation fences or sample markdown outputs. The skill consists of one SKILL.md file and contains no runnable script that could execute those blocks.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from normal content words in the skill description, user questions, and workflow text. No hash, cipher, encryption API, or cryptographic implementation appears at the flagged locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain prose about writing quality, desired length, and feedback. There is no cryptographic algorithm usage or security-sensitive operation.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md includes visible setup commands for creating a local writing workspace. This is a low-risk documentation pattern because the commands are simple and transparent, but users should review paths before running them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present exactly in the setup section. Their effect is limited to local directory and file creation.

content.value_statement: Long-form content often loses time to scattered research, weak structure, and inconsistent edits. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through outlining, citation support, drafting feedback, and final polish.
actual_capabilities: Builds article, blog, newsletter, tutorial, and thought leadership outlines.
Identifies research gaps and suggests source-backed evidence for claims.
Improves hooks with multiple opening options and reasoning.
Reviews sections for clarity, flow, evidence, and style alignment.
Formats citations as inline references, numbered references, or footnotes.
Provides final draft reviews with publishing checklists.
limitations: Requires source verification before publishing cited facts or statistics.
Does not guarantee access to paid journals or private research databases.
Depends on user-provided voice samples for accurate style matching.
Does not replace legal, medical, financial, or academic expert review.

use_cases.title: Plan a Researched Blog Post
use_cases.description: Turn a topic and target audience into a structured outline with research tasks and citation needs.

use_cases.title: Improve a Technical Tutorial
use_cases.description: Review tutorial sections for clarity, missing context, tested steps, and useful troubleshooting notes.

use_cases.title: Polish a Thought Leadership Draft
use_cases.description: Strengthen the hook, sharpen the thesis, and align evidence with a clear point of view.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create an outline for an article about [topic]. The audience is [audience], and the goal is [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Research Supporting Evidence
prompt_templates.scenario: Writer adding evidence to an outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this outline and identify claims that need evidence. Suggest credible source types and citation notes for each section.

prompt_templates.title: Improve My Introduction
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is my introduction: [paste text]. Give three stronger hook options and explain which audience each option fits best.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Editorial Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this full draft for structure, evidence, voice, citations, and publication readiness. Give prioritized edits and a final checklist.

output_examples.input: I need an article outline about AI in product management.
output_examples.output: A structured outline with a hook, introduction, three main sections, research gaps, citation needs, and a conclusion prompt.

output_examples.input: This section feels unclear and too generic.
output_examples.output: A section review covering what works, where specificity is missing, suggested rewrites, evidence gaps, and questions for the next revision.
A final review with strengths, structure notes, citation checks, readability feedback, title options, and a pre-publish checklist.

best_practices: Share the target audience, goal, tone, and publication format before drafting.
Keep a separate research file with source links, notes, and citation decisions.
Review one section at a time when the draft is still changing quickly.
anti_patterns: Do not publish statistics or expert quotes without checking the original source.
Do not ask for a complete rewrite when you need voice-preserving edits.
Do not mix unrelated audiences in one draft without clarifying priority.

faq.question: Can this skill write a full article from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes, it can help draft an article, but it works best with your topic, audience, thesis, and source preferences.

faq.question: Does it provide citations?
faq.answer: It can add citation placeholders and format references, but users should verify every source before publishing.

faq.question: Can it preserve my writing style?
faq.answer: Yes, especially when you provide examples of your previous writing and clear tone preferences.

faq.question: Is it useful for technical documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline tutorials, review explanations, add troubleshooting sections, and check whether steps are clear.

faq.question: Does it require web research?
faq.answer: No, but research-heavy work improves when the assistant can access or receive credible source material.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
